item.title: In maps: Seven days of strikes across the Middle East
item.description: Israel has continued strikes across Iran and Lebanon as Donald Trump warns action could continue for weeks

item.title: 4 Arrested in London on Suspicion of Spying on Jewish People for Iran
item.description: The police said one Iranian and three British-Iranians were detained as part of a counterterrorism investigation.

item.title: Live Updates: Israel Pummels Beirut, Intensifying Strikes on Hezbollah
item.description: Thousands fled the heaviest Israeli attacks on Lebanon’s capital since a 2024 war with the Iran-backed militia. Israel also said it was hitting Tehran, while its air defenses battled new missile launches from Iran.

item.title: Hungary confirms it is holding seven Ukrainian bank workers and $80m
item.description: The bank said they were in two vans carrying $80m (£60m) worth of cash between Austria and Ukraine.

item.title: Israel Pushes Farther Into Lebanon, and Readies for More
item.description: Days into its offensive against Hezbollah, Israel is massing armored vehicles near the Lebanese border for a potentially much larger ground incursion.

item.title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
item.description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
3 Australians Were on U.S. Submarine That Sank an Iranian Warship
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s disclosure about the attack in the Indian Ocean prompted concern that Australia’s close military cooperation with the United States could draw it into the fighting in the Middle East.

item.title: Khamenei’s Killing Sparks Anger and Grief in South Asia’s Shiite Muslims
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed during the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran, was deeply respected among the millions of Shiite Muslims in Pakistan and India.

item.title: Hong Kong pro-democracy tycoon Jimmy Lai will not appeal conviction
item.description: Lai was convicted and sentenced to 20 years in jail last month on charges for sedition and collusion with foreign forces.

item.title: Lebanon at ‘Tipping Point’ as It Seeks to Disarm Hezbollah
item.description: The country is waiting to see if the government seizes on this moment to disarm the Iranian-backed armed group and how the militants will respond.

item.title: Tears and cheers as controversial long-running Australian breakfast radio show implodes
item.description: The end of the Kyle and Jackie O Show, whose hosts had a combined A$200m contract, has shocked the media industry.

item.title: U.S. Restarts Diplomatic Relations With Venezuela
item.description: Washington has rapidly warmed up ties with Venezuela — and applied major pressure on it — since capturing the country’s president, Nicolás Maduro, two months ago.

item.title: US eases sanctions on Russian oil sales to India during Iran conflict
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent gives India a 30-day waiver to buy Russian crude as a "stop gap measure".

item.title: Lessons for central bankers from geopolitical shocks past
item.description: Higher inflation and lower investment are the norm, with the magnitude of the geopolitical shock crucial

item.title: Jamil Jivani, a Friend of JD Vance, Channels Charlie Kirk With Young Men
item.description: Jamil Jivani, a Conservative rookie member of Parliament, follows an American playbook to win over young men on college campuses.

item.title: Why vulnerable Gulf energy assets are rattling the world
item.description: Infrastructure in the region plays a critical role in global power supply

item.title: He Had a Purple Heart, PTSD and a Rap Sheet. He Had to Leave the U.S.
item.description: After a long battle with drug addiction, Sae Joon Park felt settled in a new life. But he was deported last year and is now fighting to get back to Hawaii.

item.title: The Good Times Are Over in China’s Liquor Capital
item.description: Once celebrated for its fiery spirits, the town of Maotai has reeled from a bad Chinese economy, changing tastes and a crackdown on boozy official banquets.

item.title: Israel Pounds Southern Beirut, a Hezbollah Stronghold
item.description: The strikes were the most intense bombardment since a cease-fire deal in late 2024.

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut after evacuation warning sparks panic
item.description: Huge traffic jams form after the unprecedented warning for people to leave the Hezbollah stronghold, as Israel steps up its strikes across Lebanon.

item.title: Sri Lanka takes control of second Iranian vessel a day after US sub attack
item.description: The South Asian country is caught in the middle of the US-Israeli military campaign against Iran.

item.title: After Gen-Z Revolution, Nepal Votes for a Fresh Slate of Leaders
item.description: A millennial rapper and former mayor is aiming to lead the country, six months after the old guard was ousted.

item.title: James Luckey-Lange Recounts Being Detained, Starved and Beaten in Venezuela
item.description: James Luckey-Lange, 28, wrote about kindness and shared humanity as he traveled. But he said he had been shackled, starved and beaten in Venezuela after being detained.

item.title: The young women who fought in Ethiopia's last civil war and don't want to see another one
item.description: Female fighters recall how the conflict in the northern Tigray destroyed their lives, as fears grow that fighting could resume.

item.title: 'What is the game plan?': The Iran war is unsettling China and its ambitions
item.description: China is not feeling the shock of war in the Middle East - yet. But it is feeling the ripples.

item.title: Finland plans to lift decades-old ban on hosting nuclear weapons
item.description: The government says the nation's defence environment has "significantly changed" since Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Regragui departs as Morocco appoint Ouahbi as new coach
item.description: Morocco part company with coach Walid Regragui four months before the World Cup finals, with Mohamed Ouahbi appointed in his place.

item.title: Azerbaijan furious with Iran as war spills over its border
item.description: President Ilham Aliyev says drone strikes are "act of terror" as he places armed forces on high alert.

item.title: 'We have been preparing': Why the boots on the ground in Iran could be Kurdish
item.description: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups in exile in northern Iraq tell the BBC they have plans to cross the border but deny already doing so.

item.title: Checkpoints everywhere and internet blackouts: Life in Iran as US-Israeli strikes intensify
item.description: Iranians are coping with daily strikes and security crackdowns - all while trying to stay in touch with loved ones.

item.title: Labour MP 'voluntarily suspends herself' amid China spy probe
item.description: Joani Reid said she had temporarily stood down from the party after her husband was arrested on suspicion of spying for China.

item.title: PM stands by decision not to join strikes on Iran and sends more jets to Qatar
item.description: The PM said the best way forward was a "negotiated settlement with Iran where they give up their nuclear ambitions".

item.title: Iran's high-risk war strategy seems to centre on endurance and deterrence
item.description: Tehran's approach appears to rest on a belief it can absorb strikes longer than its adversaries sustain pain and costs, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azimi.

item.title: Starmer tries to reassure as Iran crisis deepens
item.description: The PM acknowledged people are worried about family abroad, the economy, and potential further escalation.

item.title: US asked Ukraine for help fighting Iranian drones, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukraine's president says Kyiv will only help if doing so does not deplete its own air defences.

item.title: Caf postpones Wafcon 2026 just 12 days before kick-off
item.description: The 2026 Women's Africa Cup of Nations is postponed just 12 days before the finals were due to kick off in Morocco.

item.title: Conservative Anglicans pull back from electing rival to Archbishop of Canterbury
item.description: The group had said it would choose a new leader with the same title as Sarah Mullally, the first female head of the Church.

item.title: South African President on Trump’s Oval Office ‘Ambush’ and ‘Racist’ Policies
item.description: In an interview with The New York Times, President Cyril Ramaphosa opened up about the role of middle powers, relations with Washington and apartheid.

item.title: Iran school and nearby military base struck multiple times, satellite image reveals
item.description: Iranian authorities say the attack, which took place on Saturday, killed 168 people.

item.title: Ecuadorean troops find 35m-long 'narco-sub' hidden in nature reserve
item.description: Ecuador's military says the drug-trafficking submersible was loaded with fuel and "ready to go".

item.title: MP's husband and two political advisers bailed after arrests over alleged China spying
item.description: One of those arrested is the husband of Scottish Labour MP Joani Reid.

item.title: Failed asylum seeker families to be offered up to £40k to leave UK
item.description: The home secretary says the "incentives" will deliver a "significant saving to the taxpayer".

item.title: Thousands of students rally after woman's explicit images circulated
item.description: The rally was called by UCD Students' Union after a sexually explicit image was sent to hundreds of people.

item.title: China Bets on Technology to Resist U.S. Pressure
item.description: China announced a 7 percent increase in military spending and a five-year plan to try to reduce its military and industry’s reliance on Western technology.

item.title: Healey visits Cyprus after criticism of UK response to drone attacks
item.description: The Cypriot government has criticised how the UK has handled information sharing after drone attacks.

item.title: Israel and America want the Kurds to join the fight in Iran
item.description: Kurdish militias are weighing a risky bid to redraw their neighbour’s borders

item.title: Who Is Eyal Zamir, the Architect of Israel’s War Plans?
item.description: The Israeli general has long been focused on the threat from Iran and its proxies, and has called for a regional alliance against the country.

item.title: Senedd election is referendum on Starmer's leadership, says Nigel Farage
item.description: Nigel Farage helps the party launch its Senedd election manifesto, including a 1p income tax cut.

item.title: How the Danes and Swedes handle populism
item.description: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen calls an election to take on the hard right again

item.title: Meet the weekend warriors preparing to defend Europe from Russia
item.description: Lithuania’s citizen-soldiers are part of growing volunteer forces in the Baltics

item.title: Feted by Europe’s left, Spain’s Pedro Sánchez is unloved at home
item.description: The prime minister is trying to become an icon of resistance to Donald Trump

item.title: Why one of Germany’s richest regions is gripped with anxiety
item.description: Baden-Württemberg’s election is overshadowed by worries over deindustrialisation

item.title: In African development, big is beautiful again
item.description: With aid declining, grand visions for growth are back in fashion

item.title: Thousands of Africans are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: African governments are belatedly trying to stop them

item.title: Allegations against a corruption watchdog rock Malaysian politics
item.description: The prime minister, Anwar Ibrahim, repeats his predecessors’ answer

item.title: Welcome to Kashiwazaki, home to the world’s largest nuclear plant
item.description: What one small city says about modern Japan’s development

item.title: Javier Milei aggressively celebrates a string of successes
item.description: Argentina’s president has much to shout about. But job worries loom

item.title: China sets its lowest growth target for a generation
item.description: In macroeconomics, modesty is not always a virtue

item.title: China’s first railway project in the EU is open at last
item.description: Once a show of largesse, it now reflects China’s struggles on the continent

item.title: Nigel Farage and Zack Polanski: best of frenemies
item.description: Britain’s twin populists have a symbiotic relationship

item.title: AI danger gets real
item.description: The squabble between America’s government and Anthropic makes an AI disaster more likely

item.title: Binyamin Netanyahu has his war
item.description: If it goes wrong, he will also get the blame

item.title: Nepalis vote in first election since Gen Z uprising for change
item.description: This election will test whether the country is ready for a new, untested generation to shape its future.

item.title: Ethiopia experiments with 'smart' police stations that have no officers
item.description: A project to introduce unmanned sites is part of a broader adoption of digital technologies.

item.title: DR Congo government says 200 killed after landslide at rebel-held mine
item.description: The M23, which controls the coltan-rich area, has not commented and the death toll is hard to verify.

item.title: Israel Closes Al Aqsa Mosque at Ramadan, Citing Wartime Safety Concerns
item.description: The temporary measure, during the holy Muslim fasting month, follows tensions as Jewish activists increasingly encroach on the contested religious site.

item.title: Iran targets headquarters of Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq
item.description: The Iranian military's strikes come amid speculation that the US wants Iranian Kurdish groups to join its conflict with Iran.

item.title: Trying to get social care can be 'horrendous', Baroness Casey tells BBC
item.description: The chair of the independent commission on adult social care also says the care system relies on the exploitation of its workforce.

item.title: Reform leads other parties in donations ahead of May elections
item.description: The latest donations received by Nigel Farage's party includes £3m from crypto investor Christopher Harborne.

item.title: Investigative journalism in India is under threat
item.description: Politicians and tycoons are tightening their grip

item.title: Russia’s Starlink shutdown is a blow to its soldiers and drones
item.description: Launching its own satellite network is not realistic

item.title: Donald Trump must stop soon
item.description: His ill-considered conflict risks descending into chaos

item.title: It’s time to unleash Europe’s pensions
item.description: One reform offers both security in old age and dynamism now

item.title: Iran’s Friends Include China and Russia. But Where are They Now?
item.description: Iran maintains ties with a range of countries, including Turkey, India, Russia and China. Yet in this war, their support is mostly rhetoric.

item.title: Veteran Bihar chief minister to step down for move to parliament
item.description: Nitish Kumar says the state's new government will have his full cooperation and guidance.

item.title: US sub sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean, Hegseth says
item.description: More than 100 personnel were thought to be aboard the vessel, which went down to the south of Sri Lanka.

item.title: Global order is 'breaking down', Carney tells Australian parliament
item.description: The Canadian prime minister said Australia and Canada should work together as 'strategic cousins'.

item.title: China sets lowest economic growth target since 1991
item.description: It is also the first time the target has been lowered since it was cut to "around 5%" in 2023.

item.title: The Washington Roundtable on the Iran War
item.description: Is the U.S. repeating the mistakes of the invasion of Iraq?

item.title: Venezuela and US to work together on mining developments, Rodríguez says
item.description: Venezuela's interim president made the announcement after meeting with US interior minister Doug Burgum at the presidential palace in Caracas.

item.title: The devastating conflict where both sides have reasons to keep fighting
item.description: A strong supply of weapons and a history of long wars make Sudan's conflict particularly troubling.

item.title: Dirty laundry and chocolate bars: How Venezuelan prisoners smuggled messages out of jail
item.description: Prisoners recently released from detention in Venezuela describe the conditions under which they were held.

item.title: US Senate vote fails to rein in Trump war powers on Iran
item.description: The measure, passed largely along party lines, would halt US military action until approved by Congress.

item.title: Watch: Moment wolf rescued from canal in northern Italy
item.description: Video released by the Italian fire brigade shows the wolf being rescued from under a bridge.

item.title: Millions of Cubans plunged into darkness as fuel crisis deepens
item.description: Fuel shortages have been exacerbated by a US oil blockade since the seizure of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro.

item.title: How depleted weapons stockpiles could affect the Iran conflict
item.description: Weapons stocks will not alone decide the outcome of this conflict, but it's certainly a significant factor, writes the BBC's Jonathan Beale.

item.title: In times of chaos, Europe is the muddled power the world needs
item.description: In praise of the dreaded European working group

item.title: Spain's Pedro Sánchez hits back at Trump threat to sever trade saying 'no to war'
item.description: Spain's prime minister delivers a strong rebuttal to US President Donald Trump's threat to end trade with his country.

item.title: Stock markets and oil prices still volatile over fears Iran war may drag on
item.description: Experts have warned that if oil and gas prices remains elevated it could make goods and services more expensive.

item.title: The Iran war has been a stunning aerial success
item.description: Even if, at the political level, its direction is a mess

item.title: Partner of UK MP arrested on suspicion of spying for China, BBC told
item.description: The Met Police suspect three men of assisting Chinese intelligence services in the UK.

item.title: Allen century powers New Zealand to victory over South Africa in T20 World Cup semi-final
item.description: Finn Allen strikes an unbeaten century as New Zealand demolish 2024 runners-up South Africa by nine wickets to reach a second Men's T20 World Cup final

item.title: China needs a more ambitious growth target
item.description: Otherwise a fourth year of deflation awaits

item.title: Russia blames Ukrainian naval drones as tanker sinks in Mediterranean
item.description: The Arctic Metagaz went down between Libya and Malta after it was hit by explosions and a fire, Libyan officials say.

item.title: Iran postpones Khamenei funeral as US-Israeli bombardment continues
item.description: An official says more preparations are needed for the three-day ceremony in Tehran, where the supreme leader will lie in state.

item.title: Watershed moment as Russia's sporting exile ends
item.description: Sports Editor Dan Roan analyses Russia's controversial return to global sporting action at the Winter Paralympics and what it might mean for other sports.

item.title: Starmer defends Iran response as Badenoch calls for more action
item.description: It comes after Trump criticised the PM for refusing to allow the use of UK bases in the initial US-Israel strikes.

item.title: US and Ecuador forces launch operation to fight drug trafficking
item.description: Washington says the two countries are combatting 'designated terrorist organisations' in Ecuador.

item.title: The start of the Iran war was determined by spying success
item.description: But even when assassinations come off, the long-term consequences are unclear

item.title: Nigeria halts Christian pilgrimages to Holy Land over Middle East conflict
item.description: NCPC say the measure is necessary to prioritise the "safety and comfort" of Nigerian pilgrims.

item.title: Israel tells people in large parts of southern Lebanon to leave ahead of attacks
item.description: Civilians are being displaced, taking shelter in tents, parks and cars, as Israel retaliates to Hezbollah rocket attack.

item.title: Satellite images show Iranian navy and nuclear sites hit by US-Israeli strikes
item.description: US-Israeli strikes have destroyed at least 11 Iranian naval vessels, images reviewed by BBC Verify show.

item.title: Nigerian doctors suspended over death of Adichie's son
item.description: The author's 21-month-old son died in January after complications during medical procedures at a private hospital in Lagos.

item.title: Greek court upholds criminal organisation verdict on neo-Nazi Golden Dawn
item.description: The party's founder Nikos Mihaloliakos and other officials are again found guilty, almost six years after an original trial.

item.title: Mahmood to stop study visas from four countries due to 'abuse'
item.description: The government will end study visas from Afghanistan, Cameroon, Myanmar and Sudan, due to what she says is abuse.

item.title: What to know about the 'two sessions', China's biggest political meeting
item.description: The carefully choreographed annual meetings signal the priorities of the world's second-largest economy.

item.title: Lorries skipping border checks is disease risk - MPs
item.description: Government data shows a rise in trucks failing to turn up at Sevington after arrival in Kent.

item.title: Kathmandu empties as about 800,000 Nepalis head home to vote
item.description: Authorities have reported hours-long jams over the weekend while photos show buses lining highways.

item.title: South Korea woman and doctors guilty of murder of newborn baby
item.description: The case has gripped South Korea, where there is no regulation on how far into pregnancy an abortion can take place.

item.title: Japanese court upholds order to dissolve Unification Church
item.description: The church popularly known as Moonies came under scrutiny after the 2022 killing of ex-PM Shinzo Abe.

item.title: Chris Mason: Trump belittles Starmer with 'no Churchill' jibe but can the special relationship recover?
item.description: The remarks came amid a row over the refusal to permit the use of UK bases for the initial US-Israel strikes.

item.title: 'All red lines have been crossed': Gulf states weigh response to Iranian strikes
item.description: Retaliatory Iranian strikes on Gulf states have tarnished their image as safe and prosperous, writes the BBC's Barbara Plett-Usher.

item.title: Funerals held for students and staff after strike on Iran school
item.description: Iranian authorities say they were killed in a US-Israeli strike in the city of Minab on Saturday.

item.title: Trump says Starmer is 'no Winston Churchill' over Iran strikes
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer had refused to grant the US permission to use the Diego Garcia military base.

item.title: Are Gulf states running out of missile interceptors?
item.description: Their rulers insist not but they are burning through their stockpiles fast

item.title: New charges for son of Norway's crown princess on trial for rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby now faces a total of 40 charges at his trial and denies the most serious allegations.

item.title: Fears over food shortages in Tehran as residents worry about length of war
item.description: Locals tell BBC Persian they are worried about the availability of groceries and price increases as strikes continue to hit the capital.

item.title: What France’s new nuclear-arms doctrine means for Europe
item.description: Emmanuel Macron will build more warheads and include allies

item.title: Reeves says her plan is working as growth forecast cut for this year
item.description: The forecasts were made before the conflict in the Middle East broke out which could have a "very significant" impact, the OBR said.

item.title: Why Europe's leaders have struggled to speak as one on Iran
item.description: European nations say they want to work better together but have differing priorities

item.title: Battered and isolated, Hezbollah drags Lebanon into another war
item.description: Its attack on Israel in support of Iran has led to fresh mass displacements and Israeli bombing, to the fury of many Lebanese.

item.title: Cuba’s economic divides are widening
item.description: Luck and guile increasingly determine who gets how much

item.title: Go further on regulating leasehold managing agents, Rayner urges
item.description: Ex-housing secretary praises the government's draft bill but calls for more action on service charges.

item.title: We still have no idea where this war is heading
item.description: It's hard to predict how the war will end - but those fighting know how they would like it to.

item.title: Mexican drug lord 'El Mencho' buried in golden coffin
item.description: Nemesio Oseguera led the feared Jalisco New Generation Cartel until his death as a result of a firefight in February.

item.title: Why Ali Khamenei may have welcomed the nature of his death
item.description: The ideology of martyrdom could prolong Iran’s regime—and the war
